title: spyridium leucophractum
intro: Spyridium leucophractum, also known as the white rod, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to southeastern Australia and is often found in heathlands and woodlands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Spyridium leucophractum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
